subject: demo-k8s.sh -- robot heatbridge updated Inputs to heatbridge operation updated, Inputs description updated
commit_message:
demo-k8s.sh -- robot heatbridge updated
Inputs to heatbridge operation updated,
Inputs description updated

Signed-off-by: deen1985 <damian.nowak@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I96d89a7ed52850e5a9bbae2bb69177d25eb942ea
Signed-off-by: deen1985 <damian.nowak@nokia.com>
Issue-ID: INT-1584

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 7 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Just adding contributors to testsuite/oom to review this change.
I updated the heatbridge functionality within demo-ete, as a plan B compared to SO HeatBridge.

At this moment, SO HeatBridge is not working properly for me, neither for scale-out.
That`s why I think, it really makes sense to have that robot HeatBrdge as a plan B.

Tested it in my environment, works for me pretty well.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Just adding contributors to testsuite/oom to review this change.
> I updated the heatbridge functionality within demo-ete, as a plan B compared to SO HeatBridge.
> 
> At this moment, SO HeatBridge is not working properly for me, neither for scale-out.
> That`s why I think, it really makes sense to have that robot HeatBrdge as a plan B.
> 
> Tested it in my environment, works for me pretty well.

i will approve this change, but i think the code in SO is both easier to read and easier to keep track of changes needed as model changes.
Change has been successfully merged by Daniel Rose

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Just adding contributors to testsuite/oom to review this change.
> > I updated the heatbridge functionality within demo-ete, as a plan B compared to SO HeatBridge.
> > 
> > At this moment, SO HeatBridge is not working properly for me, neither for scale-out.
> > That`s why I think, it really makes sense to have that robot HeatBrdge as a plan B.
> > 
> > Tested it in my environment, works for me pretty well.
> 
> i will approve this change, but i think the code in SO is both easier to read and easier to keep track of changes needed as model changes.

Yes, agree a 100%.
Only issue for me is, that this SO HeatBridge is not really working for me (NPE related to pservers).
That`s why I need this "plan B".
